title: [FIRRTL] Infer Widths Unbreakable Loop Checking Performance Issues
body:
As reported on Matrix [[1]], there seems to be a performance bug in InferWidths. The code below hits a wall and can't get through width inference. Specifically, the performance issue is in the code that is checking for cycles in the constraints. If this code is turned off, then there is no problem getting this through InferWidths.

``` mlir
firrtl.circuit "Foo" {
  firrtl.module @Foo(in %data: !firrtl.uint<2>, out %out: !firrtl.uint<2>) attributes {convention = #firrtl<convention scalarized>} {
    %_out_WIRE = firrtl.wire : !firrtl.uint
    %_out_WIRE_1 = firrtl.wire : !firrtl.uint
    %_out_WIRE_2 = firrtl.wire : !firrtl.uint
    %_out_WIRE_3 = firrtl.wire : !firrtl.uint
    %_out_WIRE_4 = firrtl.wire : !firrtl.uint
    %_out_WIRE_5 = firrtl.wire : !firrtl.uint
    %_out_WIRE_6 = firrtl.wire : !firrtl.uint
    %_out_WIRE_7 = firrtl.wire : !firrtl.uint
    %_out_WIRE_8 = firrtl.wire : !firrtl.uint
```

``` scala
import chisel3._
import circt.stage.ChiselStage
import scala.collection._

class Foo extends RawModule {
  val data = IO(Input(UInt(2.W)))
  val out = IO(Output(UInt(2.W)))

  out := Seq.fill(64)(Wire(UInt())).foldLeft(data) { case (acc, x) =>
    x := acc ^ (acc << 1)
    x
  }

}

object Main extends App {
  println(ChiselStage.emitCHIRRTL(new Foo))
}
```
